title: Say Bismillah Before Eating
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When one of you eats, let him mention the name of Allah. If he forgets to mention the name of Allah at the beginning, let him say: Bismillahi awwaluhu wa aakhiruhu (In the name of Allah at the beginning and at the end).'
benefit: Brings blessings to your food and protects from harm

title: Eat with Your Right Hand
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When one of you eats, let him eat with his right hand, and when he drinks, let him drink with his right hand, for the Shaytan eats with his left hand and drinks with his left hand.'
benefit: Follows the Sunnah and avoids the way of Shaytan

title: Eat from What is Closest to You
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'O boy, mention the name of Allah, eat with your right hand, and eat from what is closest to you.'
benefit: Shows good manners and prevents waste

title: Don't Blow on Hot Food
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) never blew on food or drink, nor did he breathe into a vessel.
benefit: Maintains cleanliness and good manners

title: Say Alhamdulillah After Eating
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Allah is pleased with His slave when he eats something and praises Him for it, or drinks something and praises Him for it.'
benefit: Shows gratitude to Allah and earns His pleasure

title: Sleep on Your Right Side
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When you go to bed, perform ablution as you do for prayer, then lie on your right side.'
benefit: Better for health and follows the Sunnah

title: Recite Ayat Al-Kursi Before Sleeping
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When you go to bed, recite Ayat Al-Kursi, for there will remain with you a protector from Allah, and no Shaytan will approach you until morning.'
benefit: Protection from Shaytan throughout the night

title: Say the Bedtime Dua
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever recites this dua before sleeping will not die that night: Bismika Rabbi wa da'atu janbi wa bika arfa'uhu, fa in amsakta nafsi farhamha, wa in arsaltaha fahfazha bima tahfazu bihi 'ibadakas-saliheen.'
benefit: Protection and mercy from Allah

title: Greet with Assalamu Alaikum
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'You will not enter Paradise until you believe, and you will not believe until you love one another. Shall I tell you something which, if you do it, you will love one another? Spread the greeting of peace among yourselves.'
benefit: Strengthens brotherhood and earns rewards

title: Smile at Your Brother
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Smiling in the face of your brother is charity.'
benefit: Counts as charity and spreads happiness

title: Visit the Sick
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Feed the hungry, visit the sick, and free the captives.'
benefit: Shows compassion and earns great rewards

title: Help Your Brother
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Allah helps His slave as long as the slave helps his brother.'
benefit: Allah's help comes to those who help others

title: Enter with Your Right Foot
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to enter with his right foot and exit with his left foot.
benefit: Follows the Sunnah in daily actions

title: Say Bismillah Before Entering Home
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a man enters his house and mentions the name of Allah when entering and when eating, the Shaytan says to his companions: 'You will find no place to spend the night and no dinner.''
benefit: Protection from Shaytan in your home

title: Wear Clothes Starting with Right Side
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to prefer to start with the right side when putting on his shoes, combing his hair, performing ablution, and in all his affairs.
benefit: Follows the Sunnah in daily routine

title: Remove Shoes with Left Foot
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to remove his shoes starting with the left foot.
benefit: Follows the Sunnah in daily actions

title: Say Dua Before Entering Bathroom
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When entering the bathroom, say: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-khubuthi wal-khaba'ith (O Allah, I seek refuge with You from evil and evil-doers).'
benefit: Protection from evil and bad things

title: Say Dua After Leaving Bathroom
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When leaving the bathroom, say: Ghufranak (I seek Your forgiveness).'
benefit: Seeks forgiveness and shows gratitude

title: Use Miswak (Tooth Stick)
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Were it not for the fact that I did not want to make things too hard for my Ummah, I would have commanded them to use the Miswak before every prayer.'
benefit: Cleans mouth and earns rewards

title: Trim Your Nails
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Five things are from the Fitrah: circumcision, shaving the pubic hair, trimming the moustache, cutting the nails, and plucking the armpit hair.'
benefit: Maintains cleanliness and follows Fitrah

title: Say Dua Before Looking in Mirror
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to say when looking in the mirror: 'Alhamdulillah, as you have made my form beautiful, make my character beautiful as well.'
benefit: Reminds us to beautify our character

title: Say Dua When Hearing Thunder
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When you hear thunder, say: Subhanalladhi yusabbihur-ra'du bihamdihi wal-mala'ikatu min kheefatihi (Glory be to Allah, Whom the thunder glorifies with His praise, and the angels out of fear of Him).'
benefit: Remembers Allah and seeks protection

title: Say Dua When Entering Market
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever enters the market and says: La ilaha illallah wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul mulku wa lahul hamdu yuhyi wa yumeet, wa huwa hayyun la yamoot, bi yadihil khayr, wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadeer (There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise, He gives life and causes death, and He is Living and does not die, in His hand is all good, and He is Able to do all things), Allah will write for him a thousand thousand good deeds and erase from him a thousand thousand bad deeds.'
benefit: Earns great rewards and protection

title: Say Dua When Sneezing
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When one of you sneezes, let him say: Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah), and let his brother or companion say to him: Yarhamuk Allah (May Allah have mercy on you). Then let him say: Yahdeekum Allah wa yuslihu balakum (May Allah guide you and set right your affairs).'
benefit: Shows gratitude and good manners

title: Say Dua When Yawning
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When one of you yawns, let him suppress it as much as possible, for when one of you says 'Ah', the Shaytan laughs.'
benefit: Prevents Shaytan from mocking

title: Pray on Time
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) was asked: 'Which deed is the most beloved to Allah?' He said: 'Prayer at its proper time.'
benefit: Shows devotion and earns Allah’s love

title: Make Wudu Before Sleeping
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When you go to bed, perform ablution like that for the prayer, then lie on your right side.'
benefit: Ends your day in a state of purity

title: Pray in Congregation
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Prayer in congregation is twenty-seven times more rewarding than prayer alone.'
benefit: Multiplies reward and builds unity

title: Give Charity Secretly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A man who gives in charity and conceals it so that his left hand does not know what his right hand has given will be under the shade of Allah on the Day of Judgment.'
benefit: Earns Allah’s shade and sincerity

title: Avoid Backbiting
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do you know what backbiting is?' They said, 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'It is to mention about your brother something he dislikes.'
benefit: Maintains honor and avoids major sin

title: Speak Good or Remain Silent
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him speak good or remain silent.'
benefit: Prevents harm and increases wisdom

title: Guard the Tongue
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A man speaks a word, thinking it harmless, but because of it he falls into the Hellfire farther than the distance between the east and west.'
benefit: Protects from sins of the tongue

title: Be Kind to Parents
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Your mother, then your mother, then your mother, then your father.'
benefit: Earns paradise through honoring parents

title: Maintain Ties of Kinship
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever wishes for his provision to be increased and his life to be extended, let him uphold the ties of kinship.'
benefit: Brings barakah in wealth and life

title: Control Anger
hadith: A man said to the Prophet (ﷺ): 'Advise me.' He said: 'Do not become angry.' He repeated the same advice several times.
benefit: Prevents harm and leads to calmness

title: Be Just Even When Angry
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Fear Allah and be just between your children.'
benefit: Promotes fairness and avoids favoritism

title: Love for Your Brother
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.'
benefit: Strengthens Iman and community

title: Avoid Jealousy
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Beware of envy, for it devours good deeds just as fire devours wood.'
benefit: Protects your deeds and heart

title: Feed the Poor
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'O people, spread the salam, feed the hungry, and pray at night while others sleep, and you will enter Paradise in peace.'
benefit: Guaranteed path to Paradise

title: Give Water in Charity
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best form of charity is giving someone water to drink.'
benefit: A simple act with great reward

title: Pray Tahajjud
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best prayer after the obligatory ones is the night prayer.'
benefit: Brings nearness to Allah

title: Fast Voluntarily
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Fasting is a shield.'
benefit: Protects from sin and Hellfire

title: Control Your Eyes
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The glance is one of the poisoned arrows of Shaytan.'
benefit: Preserves modesty and Iman

title: Avoid Pride
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'No one who has an atom's weight of pride in his heart will enter Paradise.'
benefit: Leads to humility and acceptance

title: Say Salam to Children
hadith: Anas said: 'The Prophet (ﷺ) used to pass by children and greet them with Salam.'
benefit: Builds love and humility

title: Avoid Breaking Promises
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Three are the signs of a hypocrite: when he speaks, he lies; when he makes a promise, he breaks it; and when he is entrusted, he betrays the trust.'
benefit: Protects from hypocrisy

title: Practice Patience
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Patience is at the first strike of calamity.'
benefit: Earns Allah's reward and help

title: Seek Knowledge
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever travels a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise.'
benefit: Increases wisdom and nearness to Allah

title: Attend the Masjid Regularly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever goes to the masjid morning and evening, Allah prepares for him a place in Paradise every time he goes.'
benefit: Daily reward and closeness to Allah

title: Recite Surah Al-Mulk Every Night
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'There is a surah which contains thirty ayat which will intercede for its companion until he is forgiven: Surah Al-Mulk.'
benefit: Intercession and forgiveness

title: Pray Two Rakah After Wudu
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever performs ablution like mine and then prays two rak'ahs without letting his thoughts wander will have his past sins forgiven.'
benefit: A simple act to erase sins

title: Prolong Sujood in Prayer
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The closest that a servant comes to his Lord is when he is prostrating, so make plenty of supplication then.'
benefit: Increases nearness to Allah

title: Do Not Delay the Prayer
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The most burdensome prayers for the hypocrites are the Isha and Fajr prayers. If they knew the reward for them, they would come to them even if they had to crawl.'
benefit: Avoids hypocrisy and earns great reward

title: Give Sadaqah Regularly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Sadaqah extinguishes sin as water extinguishes fire.'
benefit: Purifies wealth and removes sin

title: Remember Allah Often
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Keep your tongue moist with the remembrance of Allah.'
benefit: Brings peace and rewards

title: Say Subhanallah 100 Times
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever says Subhanallah 100 times, a thousand good deeds are recorded for him, or a thousand sins are erased.'
benefit: Easy way to gain massive rewards

title: Say La Hawla wa la Quwwata
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah is a treasure from the treasures of Paradise.'
benefit: Earns treasures in Jannah

title: Ask Allah for Paradise
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When you ask Allah, ask Him for Al-Firdaus, for it is the highest part of Paradise.'
benefit: Aiming high in du'a earns the best reward

title: Avoid Suspicion
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the worst of false tales.'
benefit: Maintains trust and community

title: Make the First Salam
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best of the two is the one who initiates the greeting.'
benefit: Spreads peace and earns virtue

title: Help the Needy
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He is not a believer whose stomach is filled while his neighbor goes hungry.'
benefit: Shows real faith through compassion

title: Look After Orphans
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'I and the one who looks after an orphan will be like this in Paradise,' and he held his two fingers together.
benefit: Guarantees closeness to the Prophet in Jannah

title: Control Your Hands and Tongue
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A Muslim is the one from whose tongue and hand other Muslims are safe.'
benefit: Protects society and reflects real faith

title: Be Gentle in All Matters
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Gentleness is not in anything except that it beautifies it, and it is not removed from anything except that it makes it ugly.'
benefit: Gentleness brings beauty to character

title: Show Mercy to Others
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He who does not show mercy will not be shown mercy.'
benefit: Earns Allah’s mercy by showing it to others

title: Respond to the Salam
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a greeting is offered to you, reply with a better one, or at least return it equally.'
benefit: Fulfills a right of your Muslim brother

title: Speak Truth Even if Against Yourself
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Say what is true, even if it is bitter.'
benefit: Upholds justice and integrity

title: Do Not Mock Others
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'It is enough evil for a man to despise his Muslim brother.'
benefit: Preserves dignity and prevents arrogance

title: Forgive Others Often
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Be merciful to others and you will receive mercy. Forgive, and Allah will forgive you.'
benefit: Earns forgiveness and peace

title: Love for the Sake of Allah
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever loves for the sake of Allah, hates for the sake of Allah... has perfected his faith.'
benefit: Builds pure, lasting relationships

title: Shake Hands When Meeting
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When two Muslims meet and shake hands, their sins are forgiven before they part.'
benefit: Strengthens bonds and erases sins

title: Honor the Guest
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should honor his guest.'
benefit: Part of complete faith and etiquette

title: Respect the Elderly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He is not one of us who does not show mercy to our young and honor to our elderly.'
benefit: Promotes community harmony

title: Treat Your Spouse Kindly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best of you are those who are best to their wives.'
benefit: Reflects good character in private life

title: Seek Knowledge from Cradle to Grave
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.'
benefit: Increases wisdom and nearness to Allah

title: Use the Right Hand When Eating
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Eat with your right hand, and eat from what is in front of you.'
benefit: Manners and protection from Shaytan

title: Eat Together
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Eat together and do not eat separately, for the blessing is in being together.'
benefit: Brings barakah and unity

title: Clean Your Plate
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Lick your fingers and clean the dish, for you do not know in which part of the food the blessing lies.'
benefit: Respect for food and blessings

title: Cover Utensils at Night
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Cover the vessels and tie the water skins, for there is one night in the year when a disease descends and afflicts anything not covered.'
benefit: Prevents harm and follows Sunnah

title: Sleep with Wudu
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Purify these bodies and Allah will purify you, for any servant who sleeps in a state of purity has an angel that stays with him.'
benefit: Constant protection from angels

title: Wake Up with Praise
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When any one of you wakes up, let him say: Alhamdulillah alladhi ahyana...'
benefit: Begins the day with gratitude

title: Recite Surah Ikhlas Often
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, it is equivalent to one third of the Quran.'
benefit: Immense reward for short surah

title: Attend Friday Prayer
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever bathes on Friday, comes early, listens, and does not engage in idle talk, will have his sins forgiven between the two Fridays.'
benefit: Weekly purification of sins

title: Shorten the Khutbah
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Lengthening the prayer and shortening the khutbah is a sign of a man’s understanding.'
benefit: Respects time and shows wisdom

title: Send Salawat on the Prophet ﷺ
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever sends blessings upon me once, Allah will send blessings upon him tenfold.'
benefit: Earns Allah’s mercy and reward

title: Make Du'a Between Adhan and Iqamah
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The supplication between the Adhan and the Iqamah is not rejected.'
benefit: A powerful time for du'a

title: Avoid Arrogance
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever has pride in his heart equal to the weight of a mustard seed shall not enter Paradise.'
benefit: Keeps the heart humble and pure

title: Do Not Cut Off Relatives
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'No one who severs ties of kinship will enter Paradise.'
benefit: Maintains family and earns Jannah

title: Avoid Cursing Others
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A true believer is not involved in taunting or frequently cursing.'
benefit: Promotes kindness in speech

title: Pray Istikhara for Decisions
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) taught us to perform Istikhara in all matters, as he would teach a Surah from the Quran.
benefit: Seeks guidance from Allah in life decisions

title: Avoid Begging
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The upper hand is better than the lower hand. The upper hand is the one that gives, and the lower hand is the one that asks.'
benefit: Encourages self-reliance and dignity

title: Beware of the Tongue
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A man utters a word thoughtlessly, and it drags him into the Hellfire deeper than the distance between east and west.'
benefit: Caution in speech can save from Hell

title: Practice Gratitude
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He who does not thank people, does not thank Allah.'
benefit: Builds humility and gratitude

title: Return Trusts
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Render back the trust to the one who entrusted you, and do not betray even the one who betrays you.'
benefit: Fulfills amanah and earns Allah’s trust

title: Say the Morning and Evening Adhkar
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to say morning and evening: 'O Allah, I ask You for well-being in this world and the Hereafter...'
benefit: Protection throughout the day and night

title: Be Honest in Trade
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The honest and trustworthy merchant will be with the prophets, the truthful, and the martyrs on the Day of Judgment.'
benefit: Trade becomes a path to Jannah

title: Fast Mondays and Thursdays
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Deeds are presented on Mondays and Thursdays, and I love that my deeds be presented while I am fasting.'
benefit: Gains reward and builds discipline

title: Visit Graves for Reminder
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Visit the graves, for they remind you of death.'
benefit: Softens the heart and prepares for the Hereafter

title: Perform Hajj If Able
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever performs Hajj and does not commit obscenity or sin, will return as pure as the day he was born.'
benefit: Major cleansing of sins and spiritual rebirth

title: Pray Witr Before Sleeping
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Make Witr your last prayer at night.'
benefit: Completes the night prayer and earns reward

title: Avoid Extravagance
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Eat, give charity, and dress in a moderate way without arrogance or extravagance.'
benefit: Maintains balance and humility

title: Supplicate for Others in Their Absence
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The du’a of a Muslim for his brother in his absence is accepted. At his head is an angel appointed, and whenever he makes a good du’a for his brother, the angel says: Ameen, and for you the same.'
benefit: Earns blessings for yourself and others

title: Be Fair Between Children
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Fear Allah and be just among your children.'
benefit: Promotes fairness and prevents jealousy

title: Support Widows and the Poor
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The one who looks after a widow or a poor person is like the one striving in the way of Allah or fasting all day and praying all night.'
benefit: Earns immense rewards daily

title: Avoid Being Harsh
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Allah is gentle and loves gentleness.'
benefit: Gains Allah’s love and eases affairs

title: Do Not Harm Neighbors
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He is not a believer whose neighbor is not safe from his harm.'
benefit: Protects society and reflects real faith

title: Give Gifts
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Exchange gifts, you will love one another.'
benefit: Increases love and brotherhood

title: Use the Siwak
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Were it not for the hardship on my Ummah, I would have ordered them to use the siwak at every prayer.'
benefit: Promotes cleanliness and reward

title: Greet the Young and Old Alike
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The young should greet the old, the passerby the one sitting, and the few the many.'
benefit: Teaches respect and humility

title: Feed Fasting People
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever provides food for a fasting person to break his fast, he will have the same reward as him without reducing the fasting person's reward.'
benefit: Earns multiplied reward during Ramadan

title: Smile is Charity
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Your smile for your brother is charity.'
benefit: Spreads joy and earns reward

title: Don’t Lie Even in Jokes
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Woe to him who speaks and lies to make people laugh. Woe to him! Woe to him!'
benefit: Upholds truthfulness always

title: Don’t Envy Others
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do not envy one another, do not inflate prices, do not hate one another... and be, O servants of Allah, brothers.'
benefit: Promotes unity and love

title: Keep Secrets
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a man speaks a word and then turns away, it becomes a trust.'
benefit: Encourages confidentiality and trust

title: Respect Teachers
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He is not one of us who does not respect our elders, show mercy to our young, and give our scholars their due right.'
benefit: Strengthens knowledge and manners

title: Beware of Showing Off
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The thing I fear most for my nation is showing off in deeds.'
benefit: Preserves sincerity and reward

title: Say Astaghfirullah Often
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'By Allah, I seek Allah's forgiveness and repent to Him more than seventy times a day.'
benefit: Cleanses sins and brings barakah

title: Love the Quran
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best among you are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it.'
benefit: Elevates your status and rewards you immensely

title: Recite Surah Al-Kahf on Friday
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever recites Surah Al-Kahf on Friday, it will illuminate him with light from one Friday to the next.'
benefit: Weekly spiritual light and guidance

title: Don’t Belittle a Good Deed
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do not belittle any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.'
benefit: Every small deed counts

title: Guard the Five Daily Prayers
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The first matter that the slave will be brought to account for on the Day of Judgment is the prayer. If it is sound, the rest of his deeds will be sound.'
benefit: Prayer is the foundation of judgment

title: Be a Good Neighbor
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Jibril kept recommending treating neighbors with kindness until I thought he would assign a share of inheritance to them.'
benefit: Islam elevates the status of neighbors

title: Don’t Raise Voices in the Masjid
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The masjid is not for raising voices or for buying and selling.'
benefit: Maintains respect for sacred space

title: Respect the Quran
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Indeed, the one who does not honor the Qur'an is not from among us.'
benefit: Treating the Quran with reverence reflects Iman

title: Pray in Shoes if Clean
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Be different from the Jews: pray in your sandals if they are clean.'
benefit: Shows flexibility and Sunnah in prayer

title: Don’t Sleep Late Without Purpose
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) disliked sleeping before Isha and talking after it.
benefit: Preserves health and early Fajr

title: Lower Your Gaze
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do not follow a glance with another glance. The first is for you, but not the second.'
benefit: Protects the heart from fitnah

title: Make Du'a at the Last Third of Night
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Our Lord descends every night to the lowest heaven when the last third of the night remains and says: Is there anyone to call upon Me?'
benefit: One of the best times to make du'a

title: Fast on the Day of Arafah
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Fasting on the day of Arafah expiates the sins of the previous year and the coming year.'
benefit: A two-year reward for one day’s fast

title: Say La ilaha illallah Often
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best of what I and the Prophets before me have said is: La ilaha illallah.'
benefit: Most powerful form of remembrance

title: Give Zakat
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Protect your wealth by giving zakat, treat your sick by giving charity, and prepare for calamities with du'a.'
benefit: Purifies wealth and brings protection

title: Pray in Early Time
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best of deeds is prayer at its appointed time.'
benefit: Prioritizing salah increases barakah

title: Feed Animals with Kindness
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A woman was forgiven because she gave water to a thirsty dog.'
benefit: Compassion for all creatures earns forgiveness

title: Do Not Curse Time
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do not curse time, for indeed Allah is time.'
benefit: Acknowledges Allah’s control over time

title: Make Du'a When it Rains
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Two are the du'as that are never rejected: the du'a made at the time of the adhan and the du'a made when it rains.'
benefit: A special moment for answered prayers

title: Avoid Vanity in Clothing
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever drags his garment out of pride, Allah will not look at him on the Day of Judgment.'
benefit: Promotes humility and modesty

title: Keep Promises
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The signs of a hypocrite are three: when he speaks, he lies; when he promises, he breaks it; and when entrusted, he betrays.'
benefit: Keeps us away from nifaq (hypocrisy)

title: Clean the Masjid
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) praised a woman who used to clean the mosque. When she passed away, he prayed over her at her grave.
benefit: Service to the masjid is beloved to Allah

title: Avoid Extremism
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Beware of extremism in religion, for those before you were destroyed by it.'
benefit: Islam is a religion of balance and moderation

title: Do Not Enter Homes Without Permission
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'If someone peeks into your house without permission, you may put out his eye, and there is no sin upon you.'
benefit: Protects privacy and boundaries

title: Don’t Interfere in Others’ Sales
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do not undercut one another’s sales, but be brothers, O servants of Allah.'
benefit: Encourages ethical business

title: Don’t Walk with Pride
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever walks arrogantly on the earth, Allah will humble him on the Day of Judgment.'
benefit: Leads to humility and spiritual elevation

title: Make Up Missed Fasts
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever breaks a fast in Ramadan without an excuse must make it up.'
benefit: Preserves the sanctity of fasting

title: Keep Secrets of the Spouse
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The worst of people in the sight of Allah on the Day of Judgment is a man who goes to his wife, and she to him, then he spreads her secrets.'
benefit: Protects trust and privacy

title: Do Not Waste Water
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do not waste water even if you perform ablution on the bank of a flowing river.'
benefit: Encourages sustainability and gratitude

title: Seek Refuge from Hell
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever seeks refuge from Hellfire three times, Hellfire says: O Allah, protect him from the Fire.'
benefit: A simple du'a can protect from Hell

title: Keep Trusts Safe
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a man is made a ruler over the people and he dies while dishonest in his dealings, Paradise is forbidden for him.'
benefit: Accountability in leadership and trust

title: Say Salam Before Entering
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'It is not lawful for a Muslim to enter a house until he says salam.'
benefit: Promotes good manners and safety

title: Return Salam Louder
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'If someone greets you in a way you cannot hear, return it in a louder voice.'
benefit: Ensures others feel acknowledged

title: Use Your Time Wisely
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'There are two blessings which many people waste: health and free time.'
benefit: Motivates productivity and gratitude

title: Remember Death Often
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Remember often the destroyer of pleasures: death.'
benefit: Encourages preparation for the afterlife and discourages excessive worldly attachment

title: Visit the Sick
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Visit the sick, feed the hungry, and free the captive.'
benefit: Earns great rewards and shows compassion

title: Follow a Funeral Procession
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever attends the funeral of a Muslim out of sincere faith and hoping for Allah’s reward, and stays with it until it is prayed over and buried, will return with two Qirat of reward.'
benefit: Earns immense reward and reminds of the Hereafter

title: Give Glad Tidings
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Make things easy and do not make them difficult, and give glad tidings and do not repel people.'
benefit: Spreads ease and positivity in interactions

title: Avoid Excessive Laughter
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do not laugh too much, for excessive laughter deadens the heart.'
benefit: Keeps the heart soft and receptive to guidance

title: Be Truthful in Speech
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Indeed, truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to Paradise.'
benefit: Foundation of good character and path to Jannah

title: Be Modest in Dress
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Modesty is part of faith.'
benefit: Reflects inner faith and dignity

title: Recite Dhikr After Prayer
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to say after every prayer: 'Subhanallah' 33 times, 'Alhamdulillah' 33 times, and 'Allahu Akbar' 33 times, completing 100 with 'La ilaha illallah wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul mulku wa lahul hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadeer.'
benefit: Forgives sins and increases reward

title: Be Punctual
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The most beloved deeds to Allah are those that are continuous, even if small.'
benefit: Builds discipline and consistency in good deeds

title: Cleanliness is Half of Faith
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Purity is half of faith.'
benefit: Emphasizes the importance of physical and spiritual cleanliness

title: Remove Harm from the Road
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Removing a harmful thing from the road is charity.'
benefit: A simple act of kindness that earns reward

title: Be Trustworthy
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'There is no faith for one who has no trustworthiness.'
benefit: Foundation of strong character and faith

title: Visit Graveyards
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Visit the graves, for they remind you of death.'
benefit: Softens the heart and reminds of the Hereafter

title: Give Charity to Relatives First
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Charity given to the poor is charity, but charity given to a relative is two-fold: charity and maintaining kinship.'
benefit: Double reward for helping family

title: Supplicate for Your Parents
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a man dies, all his deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who prays for him.'
benefit: Continuous reward for parents even after their death

title: Guard Your Private Parts
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Guard your private parts except from your wives or those whom your right hands possess.'
benefit: Preserves chastity and earns modesty

title: Eat in Moderation
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The son of Adam does not fill any vessel worse than his stomach. A few morsels are sufficient for him to keep his back straight.'
benefit: Promotes health and avoids gluttony

title: Sleep Early, Wake Early
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) disliked sleeping before the Isha prayer and talking after it.
benefit: Good for health and facilitates waking up for Fajr

title: Listen to the Quran Attentively
hadith: Allah says in the Quran: 'So when the Qur'an is recited, listen to it and pay attention that you may receive mercy.'
benefit: Increases understanding and earns mercy

title: Fast Three Days Every Month
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Fasting three days of each month is like fasting the entire year.'
benefit: Continuous reward and spiritual purification

title: Seek Forgiveness Constantly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Glad tidings for those who find a lot of seeking forgiveness in their record.'
benefit: Cleanses sins and fills the record with good deeds

title: Remember Allah in Prosperity and Adversity
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Remember Allah during times of ease and He will remember you during times of hardship.'
benefit: Strengthens connection with Allah in all states

title: Keep the Masjid Clean
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The deeds of my Ummah were presented before me, both the good and the bad. I saw among the good deeds the removal of a harmful thing from the road. And I saw among the bad deeds mucus in the mosque not being buried.'
benefit: Earns rewards and maintains sacredness of mosques

title: Be Kind to Animals
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'There is a reward for kindness to every living thing.'
benefit: Compassion earns Allah's reward and mercy

title: Avoid Unnecessary Talk
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Indeed, among the things that I fear for you most is every eloquent hypocrite.'
benefit: Preserves sincerity and avoids hypocrisy

title: Seek Knowledge in the Morning
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'O Allah, bless my Ummah in their early mornings.'
benefit: Brings blessings to early efforts, including seeking knowledge

title: Pray Nawafil at Home
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Perform some of your prayers in your homes and do not make them graves.'
benefit: Brings blessings and light to the home

title: Don't Waste Food
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When one of you eats, he should not wipe his hand until he licks it or has it licked, for he does not know in which part of his food the blessing lies.'
benefit: Respect for Allah's provisions and avoiding waste

title: Use Perfume
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) never refused perfume.
benefit: Good hygiene and following the Sunnah

title: Recite Quran After Fajr
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to recite the Quran after Fajr prayer.
benefit: A blessed time for worship and remembrance

title: Supplicate for Guidance
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'O Allah, guide me among those whom You have guided.'
benefit: Seeking Allah's continuous guidance in life

title: Forgive and Overlook Faults
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Show forgiveness, enjoin what is good, and turn away from the ignorant.'
benefit: Promotes harmony and good character

title: Attend Congregational Prayer
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'There is no prayer for the one who does not attend the mosque.'
benefit: Emphasizes the importance of communal prayer

title: Be Humble
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever humbles himself for Allah, Allah will raise his status.'
benefit: Leads to honor and respect from Allah and people

title: Speak Softly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Indeed, Allah is gentle and loves gentleness.'
benefit: Gentle speech reflects good character and earns Allah's love

title: Give Charity Secretly and Openly
hadith: Allah says in the Quran: 'Those who spend their wealth [in Allah's way] by night and by day, secretly and publicly - they will have their reward with their Lord.'
benefit: Earns continuous reward and blessings

title: Control Your Wealth
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A time will come when a man will not care where he gets his money from, whether lawful or unlawful.'
benefit: Encourages lawful earnings and financial integrity

title: Be Thankful for Small Things
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He who does not thank people, does not thank Allah.'
benefit: Cultivates gratitude towards Allah and people

title: Maintain Good Relations with Neighbors
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Jibril kept recommending me to treat neighbors well until I thought he would make them heirs.'
benefit: Strengthens community bonds and earns reward

title: Keep Promises and Covenants
hadith: Allah says in the Quran: 'And fulfill [every] commitment. Indeed, [every] commitment will be questioned about.'
benefit: Upholds integrity and accountability

title: Be Patient in Adversity
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'No fatigue, nor disease, nor sorrow, nor sadness, nor hurt, nor distress befalls a Muslim, even if it were the prick of a thorn, but Allah expiates some of his sins thereby.'
benefit: Hardships become a means of expiating sins

title: Seek Knowledge
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever travels a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise.'
benefit: Leads to spiritual elevation, increases wisdom, and eases the path to Jannah

title: Pray Tahajjud Regularly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best prayer after the obligatory ones is the night prayer.'
benefit: Increases proximity to Allah, answers prayers, and purifies the soul

title: Be Kind to the Young
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He is not one of us who does not show mercy to our young and respect our elders.'
benefit: Fosters a compassionate and respectful community

title: Fulfill the Rights of Muslims
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The rights of a Muslim upon other Muslims are five: to return the salam, to visit the sick, to follow the funeral procession, to accept invitations, and to say 'Yarhamukallah' (May Allah have mercy on you) when one sneezes.'
benefit: Strengthens brotherhood and communal harmony

title: Avoid Disputes and Arguments
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'I guarantee a house in the surroundings of Paradise for a man who gives up arguing even if he is in the right.'
benefit: Promotes peace and avoids animosity

title: Be Generous
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Allah is generous and loves generosity.'
benefit: Earns Allah's love and blessings in wealth

title: Recite Ayatul Kursi After Every Prayer
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever recites Ayatul Kursi after every obligatory prayer, nothing will prevent him from entering Paradise except death.'
benefit: Guaranteed entry to Paradise

title: Supplicate for Others
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The supplication of a Muslim for his brother in his absence is answered. An angel is appointed at his head, and whenever he supplicates for good for his brother, the appointed angel says: 'Ameen, and for you the like thereof.''
benefit: Earns blessings for oneself and others

title: Read Quran Regularly
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Recite the Quran, for it will come as an intercessor for its people on the Day of Resurrection.'
benefit: Intercession on the Day of Judgment and spiritual guidance

title: Give Charity (Sadaqah)
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Sadaqah extinguishes sin as water extinguishes fire.'
benefit: Purifies wealth, expiates sins, and brings blessings

title: Keep the Ties of Kinship
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever wishes to have his provision expanded and his life prolonged, let him maintain his ties of kinship.'
benefit: A source of blessing in one's life and livelihood

title: Avoid Backbiting and Slander
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Do you know what backbiting is?' They said, 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'It is to mention about your brother something he dislikes.'
benefit: Preserves honor, avoids major sins, and promotes unity

title: Be Truthful
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Indeed, truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to Paradise.'
benefit: Leads to good deeds, earns trust, and is a path to Paradise

title: Visit the Sick
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Visit the sick, feed the hungry, and free the captive.'
benefit: Earns great rewards, shows compassion, and strengthens community

title: Give Good Counsel
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Religion is nasihah (sincere advice).'
benefit: Strengthens faith, promotes well-being, and earns reward

title: Be Humble (Tawadu)
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever humbles himself for Allah, Allah will raise his status.'
benefit: Leads to honor, respect, and increases closeness to Allah

title: Forgive Others
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Be merciful to others and you will receive mercy. Forgive, and Allah will forgive you.'
benefit: Earns Allah’s mercy and forgiveness, brings inner peace

title: Control Your Tongue
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him speak good or remain silent.'
benefit: Prevents sins of the tongue, earns good deeds, and promotes wisdom

title: Pray Tahajjud
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best prayer after the obligatory ones is the night prayer.'
benefit: Increases proximity to Allah, answers prayers, and purifies the soul

title: Give Water to the Thirsty
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best form of charity is giving someone water to drink.'
benefit: A simple act with immense reward, saves lives

title: Be Patient in Calamity
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Patience is at the first strike of calamity.'
benefit: Earns Allah's reward, expiates sins, and brings inner strength

title: Seek Knowledge
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever travels a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise.'
benefit: Leads to spiritual elevation, increases wisdom, and eases the path to Jannah

title: Attend Congregational Prayers
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Prayer in congregation is twenty-seven times more rewarding than prayer performed alone.'
benefit: Multiplies the reward of prayer and strengthens the community

title: Cleanliness (Taharah)
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Purity is half of faith.'
benefit: Emphasizes physical and spiritual cleanliness, earns reward

title: Remove Harm from the Road
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Removing a harmful thing from the road is charity.'
benefit: A simple act of kindness that earns immense reward, benefits society

title: Be Trustworthy
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'There is no faith for one who has no trustworthiness.'
benefit: Foundation of strong character, earns trust from Allah and people

title: Visit Graveyards for Reflection
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Visit the graves, for they remind you of death.'
benefit: Softens the heart, reminds of the Hereafter, and encourages good deeds

title: Give Charity to Relatives
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Charity given to the poor is charity, but charity given to a relative is two-fold: charity and maintaining kinship.'
benefit: Double reward for helping family, strengthens family bonds

title: Supplicate for Your Parents
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a man dies, his deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who supplicates for him.'
benefit: A continuous source of reward for parents after their death

title: Be Kind to Animals
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'There is a reward for kindness to every living thing.'
benefit: Earns reward and shows mercy to Allah's creation

title: Avoid Suspicion
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the most false of speech.'
benefit: Maintains good relations and avoids false accusations

title: Speak Truthfully
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to Paradise.'
benefit: A path to righteousness and ultimately, Paradise

title: Control Your Anger
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The strong man is not the one who can overpower others; rather, the strong man is the one who controls himself when he is angry.'
benefit: A sign of true strength and self-control

title: Be Patient
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'No one has been given a gift better and more comprehensive than patience.'
benefit: A key to enduring hardship and gaining Allah's favor

title: Give Gifts to Increase Love
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Exchange gifts, and you will love one another.'
benefit: A simple way to foster love and strengthen relationships

title: Help Those in Debt
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever grants relief to a debtor in difficulty, Allah will grant him relief in this world and the Hereafter.'
benefit: A means of attaining Allah's help and relief

title: Visit the Sick
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a Muslim visits his sick brother, he is in the midst of the fruits of Paradise until he returns.'
benefit: A great reward for a simple act of compassion

title: Follow the Funeral Prayer
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever follows a funeral procession until the prayer is offered will have a Qirat of reward, and whoever follows it until the burial is completed will have two Qirats of reward.'
benefit: An immense reward for fulfilling a communal duty

title: Be a Good Neighbor
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He is not a believer whose neighbor is not safe from his harm.'
benefit: A fundamental aspect of faith and community life

title: Honor Your Guest
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him honor his guest.'
benefit: A sign of strong faith and good character

title: Seek Knowledge
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The seeking of knowledge is obligatory for every Muslim.'
benefit: A lifelong duty that brings one closer to Allah

title: Pray in Congregation
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Prayer in congregation is twenty-seven times more rewarding than prayer performed alone.'
benefit: Multiplies the reward of prayer and strengthens the community

title: Remember Allah
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The remembrance of Allah is a cure for the hearts.'
benefit: A source of peace, tranquility, and spiritual healing

title: Read the Quran
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best of you are those who learn the Quran and teach it.'
benefit: A path to becoming the best in the sight of Allah

title: Fast Voluntarily
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever fasts one day for the sake of Allah, Allah will keep his face seventy years' distance from the Hellfire.'
benefit: A great protection from the Hellfire

title: Give Charity
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Charity does not decrease wealth.'
benefit: A means of increasing blessings and reward, not diminishing wealth

title: Maintain Family Ties
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever wishes to have his provision expanded and his life prolonged, let him maintain his ties of kinship.'
benefit: Increases lifespan and provision, strengthens family bonds

title: Be Kind to Parents
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The pleasure of the Lord is in the parent's pleasure, and the Lord's anger is in the parent's anger.'
benefit: A direct path to earning Allah's pleasure

title: Say Bismillah Before Eating
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When one of you eats, let him mention the name of Allah. If he forgets to mention the name of Allah at the beginning, let him say: Bismillahi awwaluhu wa aakhiruhu (In the name of Allah at the beginning and at the end).'
benefit: Brings blessings to your food and protects from harm

title: Eat with Your Right Hand
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When one of you eats, let him eat with his right hand, and when he drinks, let him drink with his right hand, for the Shaytan eats with his left hand and drinks with his left hand.'
benefit: Follows the Sunnah and avoids the way of Shaytan

title: Eat from What is Closest to You
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'O boy, mention the name of Allah, eat with your right hand, and eat from what is closest to you.'
benefit: Shows good manners and prevents waste

title: Don't Blow on Hot Food
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) never blew on food or drink, nor did he breathe into a vessel.
benefit: Maintains cleanliness and good manners

title: Say Alhamdulillah After Eating
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Allah is pleased with His slave when he eats something and praises Him for it, or drinks something and praises Him for it.'
benefit: Shows gratitude to Allah and earns His pleasure

title: Sleep on Your Right Side
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When you go to bed, perform ablution as you do for prayer, then lie on your right side.'
benefit: Better for health and follows the Sunnah

title: Recite Ayat Al-Kursi Before Sleeping
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When you go to bed, recite Ayat Al-Kursi, for there will remain with you a protector from Allah, and no Shaytan will approach you until morning.'
benefit: Protection from Shaytan throughout the night

title: Say the Bedtime Dua
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever recites this dua before sleeping will not die that night: Bismika Rabbi wa da'atu janbi wa bika arfa'uhu, fa in amsakta nafsi farhamha, wa in arsaltaha fahfazha bima tahfazu bihi 'ibadakas-saliheen.'
benefit: Protection and mercy from Allah

title: Greet with Assalamu Alaikum
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'You will not enter Paradise until you believe, and you will not believe until you love one another. Shall I tell you something which, if you do it, you will love one another? Spread the greeting of peace among yourselves.'
benefit: Strengthens brotherhood and earns rewards

title: Smile at Your Brother
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Smiling in the face of your brother is charity.'
benefit: Counts as charity and spreads happiness

title: Visit the Sick
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Feed the hungry, visit the sick, and free the captives.'
benefit: Shows compassion and earns great rewards

title: Help Your Brother
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Allah helps His slave as long as the slave helps his brother.'
benefit: Allah's help comes to those who help others

title: Enter with Your Right Foot
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to enter with his right foot and exit with his left foot.
benefit: Follows the Sunnah in daily actions

title: Say Bismillah Before Entering Home
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a man enters his house and mentions the name of Allah when entering and when eating, the Shaytan says to his companions: 'You will find no place to spend the night and no dinner.''
benefit: Protection from Shaytan in your home

title: Wear Clothes Starting with Right Side
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to prefer to start with the right side when putting on his shoes, combing his hair, performing ablution, and in all his affairs.
benefit: Follows the Sunnah in daily routine

title: Remove Shoes with Left Foot
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to remove his shoes starting with the left foot.
benefit: Follows the Sunnah in daily actions

title: Say Dua Before Entering Bathroom
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When entering the bathroom, say: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-khubuthi wal-khaba'ith (O Allah, I seek refuge with You from evil and evil-doers).'
benefit: Protection from evil and bad things

title: Say Dua After Leaving Bathroom
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When leaving the bathroom, say: Ghufranak (I seek Your forgiveness).'
benefit: Seeks forgiveness and shows gratitude

title: Use Miswak (Tooth Stick)
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Were it not for the fact that I did not want to make things too hard for my Ummah, I would have commanded them to use the Miswak before every prayer.'
benefit: Cleans mouth and earns rewards

title: Trim Your Nails
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Five things are from the Fitrah: circumcision, shaving the pubic hair, trimming the moustache, cutting the nails, and plucking the armpit hair.'
benefit: Maintains cleanliness and follows Fitrah

title: Say Dua Before Looking in Mirror
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) used to say when looking in the mirror: 'Alhamdulillah, as you have made my form beautiful, make my character beautiful as well.'
benefit: Reminds us to beautify our character

title: Dua When It Rains
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When it rains, say: Allahumma sayyiban nafi'an (O Allah, make it a beneficial rain).'
benefit: Turns rain into a blessing

title: Control Your Gaze
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The zina of the eyes is looking (at what is forbidden).'
benefit: Protects from temptation

title: Avoid Suspicion of Pious People
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Avoid suspicion, for suspicion is the most false of tales.'
benefit: Maintains trust and unity

title: Be Kind to Your Servants
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'They are your brothers and your servants whom Allah has placed under your authority. So, if a man has his brother under him, he should feed him from what he eats and dress him from what he wears.'
benefit: Promotes justice and compassion

title: Pray Two Rak'ahs When Entering Masjid
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'If any one of you enters a mosque, he should pray two rak'ahs before sitting.'
benefit: Honors the mosque and earns reward

title: Dua After Adhan
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever says after hearing the adhan: 'Allahumma Rabba hadhihi-d-da'wati-t-tammah... my intercession for him will be permitted on the Day of Resurrection.'
benefit: Earns the Prophet's intercession

title: Be Humble in Your Dealings
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Whoever humbles himself before Allah, Allah will raise him.'
benefit: Leads to honor from Allah

title: Avoid Cursing
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The believer is not a slanderer, nor a curser, nor is he obscene or vulgar.'
benefit: Maintains pure and respectful speech

title: Be Patient with Your Family
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The best of you is the one who is best to his family, and I am the best of you to my family.'
benefit: A sign of true excellence

title: Dua When Leaving Home
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'When a man leaves his house and says: Bismillah, tawakkaltu 'alallah, wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah... it will be said to him: You are guided, defended, and protected.'
benefit: Provides divine protection

title: Give Your Body Its Right
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Your body has a right over you.'
benefit: Encourages self-care and health

title: Use Pleasant Names
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The most beloved of your names to Allah are 'Abdullah and 'Abdur-Rahman.'
benefit: Encourages beautiful and meaningful names

title: Avoid Sleeping on Your Stomach
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'This is a posture that Allah dislikes.'
benefit: Promotes healthy sleeping habits

title: Be Grateful for Blessings
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'He who does not thank the people does not thank Allah.'
benefit: Builds a grateful heart

title: Avoid Imitating the Opposite Gender
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) cursed men who imitate women and women who imitate men.
benefit: Maintains natural gender roles

title: Be Merciful to the Creation
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'The merciful are shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Be merciful to those on the earth, and the One in the heavens will be merciful to you.'
benefit: Invites Allah's mercy

title: Avoid Gossiping (Namimah)
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'A gossip-monger will not enter Paradise.'
benefit: Protects relationships and unity

title: Make Peace Between People
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'Shall I not tell you of something that is better than the status of fasting, prayer, and charity?' They said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Making peace between people.'
benefit: A highly rewarding act

title: Be Easy-Going in Business
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'May Allah have mercy on a man who is lenient when he sells, when he buys, and when he demands his due.'
benefit: Brings blessings to transactions

title: Memorize the Quran
hadith: The Prophet (ﷺ) said: 'It will be said to the companion of the Qur'an: 'Read and ascend, and recite as you used to recite in the world, for your status will be at the last verse you recite.'
benefit: Elevates one's status in Paradise
